clinical_history: ECG from a middle aged woman not on any medications known to affect the ECG. Which ONE of the following abnormalities is most likely present based on these waveforms?
diagnosis: The ECG shows borderline resting sinus tachycardia at 95/min. Most notable, and relatively subtle in some leads, is the prolongation of repolarization (QT-U) with prominent U waves that are best seen in the precordial leads (e.g., V2). The serum potassium was 2.4 mEq/L. The patient was diabetic with a serum glucose of 462 mg/dL at the time of this ECG.
